results.title: City in the sun
results.creator_credit: Alasdair Loch and Australian news and Information Bureau
results.brief_description: Inspired by Arne Sucksdorff’s “Manniskor i Stad” (Rhythm of a city), this film reflects the mood of a day in the life of Sydney, New South Wales, from morning until night.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Alasdair Loch and Australian news and Information Bureau

results.title: Citizen soldier
results.creator_credit: Greg Reading, Australian Commonwealth Film Unit and Telemark Productions
results.brief_description: An impression of the life throughout the year of a citizen soldier in the Citizens Military Forces. It delves into such aspects as how effective CMF training really is, why it is important to the individual and the country and what it offers to the soldier and to his employer.

results.title: The Change at Groote
results.creator_credit: Stefan Sargent, Richard Mason and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: Designed to show how the aboriginal people of Groote Eylandt in the Gulf of Carpentaria are adjusting to the new life brought about by the discovery on their lands of vast manganese deposits which are now being mined by Broken Hill Proprietary. Made as a fragmented collage of images and sounds, it is intended to produce a direct emotional response, and suggest the confused values of the aboriginals undergoing a social and cultural revolution.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Stefan Sargent, Richard Mason and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit

results.title: Careering into marriage
results.creator_credit: Susan Doring, Robin Hughes and Film Australia
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Working series”.
One of a series of films about attitudes to work. Stresses the importance of career planning for girls, even though they may marry and leave work permanently or have a broken career. Many women become the breadwinners of their family, and others manage to successfully combine career and family.

results.title: Cane cutters
results.creator_credit: Hugh McInnes, John Heyer and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: One of the “Interesting Australians” series which showed Australians at work. The first person, poetic commentary describes the life and work of cane cutters and celebrates their mateship and their sense of belonging to ‘the good earth of tropical Australia’.

results.title: Bullocky
results.creator_credit: Richard Mitchell, Gil Brealey and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: Vic Deaves is a fourth generation bushman. For as long as he can remember, his family has lived in and off the Australian bush, often as timber-getters in the coastal valleys of New South Wales. Vic is a bullocky, one of the last of his kind. In this film Vic talks about his life and work, his bullocks and his friends. The area where he works is very much a part of another Australia, now past, but Vic and his surroundings represent the best of it.

results.title: The Billiard room
results.creator_credit: Peter Weir, Joe Scully, Australian Commonwealth Film Unit and Film Australia
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Adults Learning”.
Designed to promote group discussion on the problems of the adult learning process in management-staff relations through a discussion between a potential university dropout, his friend and an onlooker. It suggests that not all learning occurs in the classroom. This film should not be screened by itself as a documentary. It does not provide direct information on the process of adult learning. It is a teaching aid which provides a basis for discussion.
